steps.title: Determine whether the graph is linear or exponential
steps.content: A linear function produces a straight line; an exponential function produces a curve. The graph shows a straight line, so the function is linear. This eliminates C (increasing exponential) and D (decreasing exponential).

steps.title: Determine the direction of change
steps.content:
Reading left to right, the line rises — as $x$ increases, $y$ increases. The function is therefore increasing, not decreasing. This eliminates B (decreasing linear).
  • A: Increasing linear — straight line rising left to right. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: Decreasing linear — line would fall left to right. Eliminated.
  • C: Increasing exponential — would be a rising curve. Eliminated.
  • D: Decreasing exponential — would be a falling curve. Eliminated.
